issues: verifier marked attempt as not passed

verifier_issues.description: The grader output contains conflicting status/score fields: deterministic_precheck shows final_score=1.0 and artifact_contract_passed=true, top-level 'score' is 1.0 and 'passed' is true in some parts of the record, while the model grader final_score is 0.25 and several nested sections (raw_llm_output, raw_model_output) report passed=false. These contradictions are internal to the grader result and make it unclear which value is authoritative.
verifier_issues.title: Inconsistent pass/score fields

verifier_issues.description: The model grader claims missing required artifacts 'output_schema.json' and 'acceptance_checks.md' as a failure mode. The deterministic precheck and actual_outputs list expected_deliverable_items only as ['PBPK_QC_2.xlsx', 'expert_memo.md'] and produced those two artifacts. There is no trace/evidence in actual_outputs or artifact previews that the grader was provided with or required output_schema.json or acceptance_checks.md, so the claim that those are missing is unsupported by the provided trace and artifact previews.
verifier_issues.title: Unsupported claim about missing artifacts

verifier_issues.description: The model explicitly notes it could not open PBPK_QC_2.xlsx due to missing openpyxl and relied on the expert_memo.md text preview for semantic checks. This reliance is supported by artifact_content_previews which show 'openpyxl_unavailable' and the expert_memo.md text preview. The grader correctly flagged the parsing limitation, but any semantic claims about the XLSX contents beyond what appears in the memo are therefore not grounded in the parsed XLSX and should be labeled as provisional. The grader does acknowledge this limitation, but some penalty statements imply missing files inside the workbook that were not verifiable.
verifier_issues.title: Reliance on artifact text preview where workbook parsing failed

verifier_issues.description: The model assigns a final semantic/model score of 0.25 and states reasons (missing required artifacts, unverifiable formulas) but does not provide a clear mapping between each rubric criterion and the numerical deduction leading to 0.25. Deterministic rubric item ri_1 is shown as passed= true in the deterministic precheck but as failed/score=0.25 in the model rubric item; the relationship between these rubric evaluations is not made explicit.
verifier_issues.title: Score rationale lacks explicit mapping to rubric items

extra_model_fields.verifier_issues_summary: The grader correctly detects an inability to parse the XLSX and relies on the memo preview; however, it reports missing artifacts that are not required according to the deterministic precheck/actual_outputs and it contains inconsistent pass/score fields across nested sections. Scores and pass/fail flags should be reconciled and claims about missing deliverables should be supported by the trace or rubric.

metadata_json.public_response_summary: Model verifier checked grounding and score consistency.

passed_checks: artifact_presence_verification (deterministic precheck confirms PBPK_QC_2.xlsx and expert_memo.md present)
artifact_preview_consistency (expert_memo.md preview matches claims in grader reasoning)

recommendations: Reconcile and normalize the authoritative score and pass/fail flag at the top level and in nested raw outputs so consumers have a single source of truth.

If the rubric requires additional artifacts (e.g., output_schema.json, acceptance_checks.md), include that requirement explicitly in deterministic_precheck.expected_deliverable_items so the deterministic precheck and model grader align.

When spreadsheet parsing fails, explicitly limit semantic deductions to content visible in text previews and mark any workbook-specific claims as unverifiable; avoid asserting missing files inside the workbook unless listed in actual_outputs or artifact manifest.
Provide a clear numeric mapping from rubric items to the final score (show deductions per rubric item) so the scoring pathway is auditable.

issues: verifier marked attempt as not passed

extra_model_fields.verifier_issues_summary: Grader conclusions are supported by the provided actual_outputs and artifact previews: required artifacts (agent_trace.json, deliverables) are missing and logs show model/runtime capacity errors. The final_score=0.0 and artifact_contract_score=0.0 are consistent with the deterministic precheck and preserved logs. No evidence of hidden-reference leakage or unsupported claims was found.

metadata_json.public_response_summary: Model verifier checked grounding and score consistency.

grounding_evidence: artifact_content_previews: attempts/revised/prompt.md (task contract and required outputs listed)
extra_model_fields.notes: Deterministic precheck confirms missing required artifacts; semantic correctness cannot be evaluated without the deliverables. Logs explicitly show model capacity/runtime errors that explain the failure to produce artifacts.

raw_llm_output.verifier_issues_summary: Grader conclusions are supported by the provided actual_outputs and artifact previews: required artifacts (agent_trace.json, deliverables) are missing and logs show model/runtime capacity errors. The final_score=0.0 and artifact_contract_score=0.0 are consistent with the deterministic precheck and preserved logs. No evidence of hidden-reference leakage or unsupported claims was found.

grounding_evidence: artifact_content_previews: attempts/revised/prompt.md (task contract and required outputs listed)
extra_model_fields.notes: Deterministic precheck confirms missing required artifacts; semantic correctness cannot be evaluated without the deliverables. Logs explicitly show model capacity/runtime errors that explain the failure to produce artifacts.

raw_model_output.verifier_issues_summary: Grader conclusions are supported by the provided actual_outputs and artifact previews: required artifacts (agent_trace.json, deliverables) are missing and logs show model/runtime capacity errors. The final_score=0.0 and artifact_contract_score=0.0 are consistent with the deterministic precheck and preserved logs. No evidence of hidden-reference leakage or unsupported claims was found.
